meta.name: SPARTA QRA Annotation Redesign Review
meta.description: Compare the live SPARTA QRA evidence annotation surface against the supplied QRA queue/evidence trace mockup. The target is a dense operator review UI, not a generic dashboard.

surface.role: High-throughput QRA annotation page for reviewing source-grounded evidence cases, failed gates, advisory repair guidance, and human approve/reject/correct/escalate actions.
must_support: Reviewer can identify the selected QRA and its queue status immediately.
Reviewer can see concrete CAPEC-649/T1036.006 evidence details without expanding generic process boxes.
Source-grounded evidence must be visually separated from advisory/persona repair reasoning.
Approval must remain blocked when evidence gates are unresolved, failed, or adversarial.
The visual direction should follow the supplied mockup: left queue, central decision/evidence trace, pinned action bar, and useful iconography.

layout.reference: 4-column concept: icon rail, queue, main decision/evidence trace, right evidence-flow/summary panel.
layout.currentConstraint: Existing UX Lab shell adds an outer project rail; review should distinguish unavoidable shell chrome from QRA surface defects.
layout.criticalGap: Current implementation should look and behave closer to the supplied annotation mockup than to a generic accordion report.

interactions.keyboard: Visible focus rings, queue row selection, shortcuts for accept/reject/edit/navigation.
interactions.primaryActions: Approve blocked, Reject, Retain fixture or Correct and rerun, Escalate.
interactions.trace: Rows use meaningful lucide icons and expand/collapse affordances; summary nodes jump to corresponding trace rows.
